mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance depresses equity valuations by raising the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, which depresses aggregate demand and equity valuations over a 60-day forward horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of months where PAYEMS declines month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) combined with expanding real GDP (GDPC1) signals robust consumer demand and economic growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with sustained demand growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of periods where both RSAFS and GDPC1 are rising, or joint interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below its 12-month moving average, signaling supply-side weakness and reduced corporate profitability, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite INDPRO below 12-month MA, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession phase
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite UNRATE rising > 0.3pp in 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) exceed 1,300 thousand units annualized while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains stable month-over-month, signaling healthy residential demand without overheating, which supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through construction and consumer wealth effects.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1,300 thousand and CPIAUCSL month-over-month change < 0.5%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or interaction term between HOUST and CPIAUCSL is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is below 4.0% and declining, combined with year-over-year CPI (CPIAUCSL) growth below 3.5%, monetary conditions are accommodative and inflation-controlled, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as real discount rates compress.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 4.0% and declining, CPIAUCSL YoY growth < 3.5%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite accommodative monetary conditions, or joint interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) grows at an annualized rate exceeding 2% over a 3-month period while payroll employment (PAYEMS) simultaneously expands, signaling robust supply-side productivity and labor utilization that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through earnings growth.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth > 2% and PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with productivity gains
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite INDPRO and PAYEMS both expanding, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows.
